description: Phase 10 T2 spike: does Apple-AMX INT8 (via CoreML/BNNS, the only non-deprecated AMX route) beat our SDOT? Decisive answer: NO. CoreML predict latency, CPU_ONLY (= BNNS -> AMX), fc1 650M shape M=2048 K=1280 N=5120, fresh random input per call (no prediction caching).

our_kernels_same_shape_est_ms.note: P9 amx_spike (M=512): SDOT 6.41, cblas-fp32 4.95; x4 for M=2048 -> ~25.6 / ~20.6, consistent with the fp32-pinned 20.61 measured here.

findings.amx_int8_verdict: DEAD. W8A8 (13.95ms) is SLOWER than fp16 (11.30) and weight-only-int8 (10.88). BNNS dequantizes int8 to fp16 and runs a float kernel; activation int8 quant/dequant just adds overhead. There is no int8 matmul speedup on Apple AMX through any accessible path (deprecated BNNSMatMul int8 = rc=-1 in P9; BNNSGraph = CoreML-artifact runtime that dequantizes).
findings.amx_fp16_finding: fp16 (11.30ms) is ~1.8x our cblas-fp32 (20.6) and ~2.3x our SDOT (~25.6). CoreML downcasts to fp16 by default (fp32-pinned 20.61 vs fp16 11.30 confirms AMX-fp16 ~1.8x AMX-fp32). fp16 is the real AMX lever — NOT int8.
findings.fp16_accessibility: Accelerate exposes NO half-precision GEMM (cblas_{s,d,c,z}gemm only). The fp16 AMX win is reachable only via the heavy CoreML/BNNSGraph artifact pipeline (per-shape mlmodelc + BNNSGraphContextExecute) + fp16 numerical validation. No trivial cblas_hgemm shortcut.
findings.implication: The committed 'BNNSGraph AMX-INT8' headline cannot deliver — int8 gives zero AMX speedup. The only AMX speedup is fp16 (~2.3x vs SDOT), at the cost of the same heavy pipeline + fp16 quality risk. Escalated to the user; user chose to build the fp16 pipeline.

direct_c_api_fp16_probes_ms_M2048.conclusion: The fast ~11ms fp16 AMX path is reachable ONLY via the CoreML->mlmodelc->BNNSGraph compiled-graph pipeline. Direct C calls don't reach it: BNNSMatMul fp16 works but doesn't pre-pack the weight (17.3ms, only 1.13x cblas); the classic FullyConnected filter (which WOULD pre-pack) returns NULL for fp16 (fp32-only). So weights must be graph constants in a compiled mlmodelc for BNNS to pre-pack into AMX layout. No direct-call shortcut. Remaining linchpin to prove: C++ BNNSGraphCompileFromFile + BNNSGraphContextExecute hits ~11ms.
